title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry scalp

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The aloe vera gel extracted from the plant contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that deeply nourish the scalp, calming irritation and flakiness. As it penetrates the skin, it hydrates the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness, while also promoting healthy hair growth. By using an herbal aloe tincture on a regular basis, individuals with dry scalp can achieve a healthier, more balanced scalp environment that is better equipped to support overall hair health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to dryness, itchiness, and flaking.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe tinctures, like vitamin C and E, help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing oxidative stress that can lead to dry scalp issues.

Moisturizing properties: Aloe's natural moisturizers, including glycoproteins and polysaccharides, help to lock in moisture and soothe the scalp, alleviating dryness, itchiness, and irritation while promoting healthy skin hydration.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a type of anthraquinone that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry scalp.

Aloe-vera-polysaccharides: These complex sugars have been shown to stimulate the production of ceramides, an essential component of the skin's barrier function, which can help lock in moisture and reduce dryness and flakiness on the scalp.

Cinnamyl-alcohol: A phenolic compound found in Aloe vera, cinnamyl-alcohol has been reported to possess antifungal and antimicrobial properties, helping to control fungal infections and other microbial imbalances that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Leaves: The extract from Aloe vera leaves also contains aloin, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth on the scalp.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaf extract has been shown to have anti-fungal and antibacterial properties, helping to control scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe vera leaves from your garden or purchase from a local supplier weighing approximately 500g.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with lukewarm water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and macerate them in a 1:2 ratio with 95% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid from solids.
Store the aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to mature and age properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties, which combat the root causes of dryness and flakiness. The tincture's oil extracts from the leaves of the Melaleuca alternifolia plant deeply penetrate the scalp, soothing irritated skin and reducing inflammation. As a result, tea tree tinctures promote a healthy scalp environment, regulating oil production and preventing flakes from forming. This natural remedy effectively treats dry scalp conditions, leaving hair feeling soft, smooth, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antifungal Properties: Tea tree oil has potent antifungal properties that help to combat fungal infections on the scalp, such as dandruff and ringworm, which can contribute to a dry scalp.

Tea Tree Oil's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with a dry scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
Camphor's Soothing and Moisturizing Properties: Camphor, a key component of tea tree oil, helps to soothe and moisturize the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness associated with a dry scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): As an antimicrobial agent, cineole helps combat scalp infections and inflammation that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Limonene: A natural solvent with antifungal properties, limonene helps reduce fungal growth on the scalp, which can cause irritation and dryness.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions, while also promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used due to their high oil content, particularly cineole, which is known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are also used, as they contain a similar composition of oils to the leaves, providing similar benefits for the scalp.
Barks: Barks are occasionally used, as they contain some of the same therapeutic compounds as the leaves and stems, although in smaller amounts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia in the early morning when they are at their highest essential oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven or with a food dehydrator to prevent bacterial growth.
Combine 1 part of the dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep it for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The antiseptic qualities of lavender help to soothe and reduce redness, while also fighting off any underlying fungal or bacterial infections that may be contributing to the dryness. Additionally, lavender's moisturizing benefits can help to lock in moisture and calm the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness. As a natural remedy, English lavender tinctures provide a gentle yet effective solution for soothing and treating dry scalp issues.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: These properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry, itchy scalp conditions by reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tincture can help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry scalp, promoting a healthier scalp environment.

Soothing and calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help to ease stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for dry scalp, and also provide a soothing, relaxing effect on the scalp itself, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate scalp irritation and itchiness.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene ester that contributes to the antiseptic and antifungal properties of Lavandula angustifolia, helping to control scalp infections and prevent further irritation.
Lavandulol: A terpene that exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment, reducing dryness and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: They are used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to retain the plant's essential oils and provide a concentrated source of its therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's essential oils stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy circulation and reducing flakiness. Additionally, rosemary tincture's antioxidant compounds help protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by styling products, leaving it feeling nourished and refreshed. As a result, rosemary tinctures provide relief for dry, itchy scalps, promoting a healthy and balanced hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, help to soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, reducing redness and itchiness associated with a dry scalp.

Stimulating properties: Rosemary's stimulating effects can help to increase blood flow and circulation to the scalp, which can improve the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with dry scalp by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections that may contribute to dry scalp, such as dandruff or seborrheic dermatitis.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and protect the scalp from further irritation and infection, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils that help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Flowers: Employed for their anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing scalp irritation and promoting healthy hair growth.
Stems: Utilized for their ability to provide a cooling effect on the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning when they are at their most potent.
Clean and dry the rosmarinus officinalis leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and ensure quality.
Combine one part rosmarinus officinalis leaves with one part high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's natural compounds work together to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and flakiness associated with a dry scalp. Additionally, thyme's ability to stimulate blood flow promotes healthy hair growth by increasing oxygenation and nourishment to the scalp, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and manageable. As a result, using herbal thyme tinctures can bring relief from dry scalp symptoms, promoting a healthy and vibrant head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal infections, such as malassezia, which can contribute to dry scalp conditions like dandruff and itchiness.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, including thymol and carvacrol, reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with a dry scalp, promoting soothing relief and calming the skin.
Antifungal properties: Thyme's antifungal properties help regulate the natural fungal balance of the scalp, preventing overgrowth that can lead to flaking, itching, and dryness.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: A phenolic compound that acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Carvacrol: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalp skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry scalp.
Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of thymol, an antifungal compound that helps combat scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reducing dryness and irritation.
Buds: Thyme buds are often used for their antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help eliminate scalp infections and promote a balanced scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a pesticide-free area at dawn when dew is still present.
Chop the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped thymus vulgaris leaves with 50% vodka by volume in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help to reduce flaking, itchiness, and redness associated with a dry scalp. Additionally, sage's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and hydrate the scalp, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and healthy-looking. Regular use of sage tincture can also promote healthy hair growth and improve overall scalp health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause scalp infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's high antioxidant content can help protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall scalp health and vitality.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, thus alleviating dry scalp symptoms.
Salvianolic acid B: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, making it an effective remedy for dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are the most commonly used part to make tinctures for a dry scalp due to their high concentration of essential oils that promote scalp health.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain a significant amount of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and protect the scalp from irritation and dryness.
Buds: The buds of Salvia officinalis are rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with a dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 250-500 grams of dried salvia officinalis with 750-1000 ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The aloe-like substance in chamomile, apigenin, binds to skin cells, reducing redness and itching associated with dry scalp conditions. Additionally, chamomile's antifungal and antibacterial properties help regulate the scalp's natural balance, preventing fungal and bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to flaking and itchiness. By promoting a healthy scalp environment, chamomile tinctures gently alleviate symptoms of dry scalp, leaving the scalp feeling calm, soothed, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with a dry scalp, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and flakiness.

Soothing and calming effects: The apigenin in chamomile tincture has a sedative effect on the scalp, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related scratching and irritation that can exacerbate a dry scalp.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: Apigenin is a flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalp, providing relief from dry scalp irritation.
Bisabolol: Bisabolol is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to calm and protect the scalp from irritation and infection associated with dry scalp.
α-Bisabolol oxide: α-Bisabolol oxide is a sesquiterpene that has antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to control the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high essential oil content, particularly chamazulene and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties for skin irritations.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the flowers and are often used to create a stronger tincture for addressing scalp conditions.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant also contain essential oils that can help soothe and calm the scalp, although they are less commonly used than the flowers and seeds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature flowers in their full bloom and dry them completely to preserve their medicinal properties for up to a year.
Combine 1 part dried flowers with 2 parts vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar for a strong tincture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction and steeping.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.
Label and date the tincture bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its remarkable ability to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of geranium tincture help to reduce dandruff and flaking, while its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated scalps. Additionally, geranium's regenerative properties promote healthy hair growth by stimulating blood flow to the scalp, resulting in a hydrated and balanced scalp that is better equipped to manage dryness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Geranium tincture's antifungal compounds help combat fungal infections, such as malassezia, which can contribute to dry scalp conditions like dandruff and itchiness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Geranium tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
Antioxidant properties: Geranium tincture's antioxidants help protect the scalp from oxidative stress, damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, flakiness, and hair loss.
remedy_constituents: Geraniol: Helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth, while also exhibiting antifungal properties to combat scalp infections.
Linalool: Contributes to soothing and calming the scalp, reducing irritation and redness associated with dry scalp conditions, and may also help to regulate the scalp's natural pH balance.

Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits potent antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage, while also reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly geraniol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their fragrance and essential oils, which help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
Stems: The stems are used for their essential oils, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce irritation and promote healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Dry the plant material in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Use a coffee grinder to grind the dried material into a fine powder and store it in an airtight container.
Combine 250ml of 95% ethanol with the plant powder in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its invigorating properties that stimulate blood flow and soothe irritation. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to loosen and remove flakes, reducing itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with dry scalps. As a result, peppermint tinctures can help to restore balance and health to the scalp, leaving it feeling cool, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with a dry scalp.
Cooling and soothing: The menthol content in peppermint tinctures has a natural cooling effect that can help calm and soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, providing relief from discomfort.
Antifungal and antibacterial: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties can help combat fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, which can contribute to dryness, flakiness, and other scalp issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the scalp, which can help reduce inflammation and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
Menthone: Menthone, a monoterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Mentha phenolic acids (such as rosmarinic acid): Mentha phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain menthol and menthone, which help to cool and soothe the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions.
Stems: They are rich in antioxidants and menthol, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp circulation.
Roots: They contain menthone and limonene, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help to control scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day.
Clean the leaves and flowers thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 250 grams of the herb in 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and keep it in a cool place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tincture's antifungal and antibacterial compounds also help to combat infections that can contribute to flaking and itchiness. Additionally, Kansas coneflower is rich in antioxidants, which promote healthy hair growth and reduce oxidative stress. By incorporating a Kansas coneflower tincture into your skincare routine, you can effectively tackle dry scalp issues and enjoy a healthier, more radiant mane.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Kansas Coneflower's bioactive compounds, such as echinacein and caryophyllene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, which can contribute to a dry, itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin balance.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols and flavonoids present in Kansas Coneflower tinctures have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with a dry scalp, promoting a more comfortable and healthy scalp environment.

Antioxidant properties: Echinacea's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells and contribute to dryness and irritation, protecting the scalp from oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, which can contribute to a more balanced scalp environment.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia may have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm an irritated scalp, potentially alleviating dryness and itchiness.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in this plant may support the immune system and promote skin integrity, which can help protect the scalp from damage and promote a healthy environment to combat dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used for tinctures due to their high concentration of echinacoside and alkaloids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties beneficial for scalp health.
Leaves: Leaves are also commonly used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm dry scalp irritations.
Stems: Stems are another part often used for their purported antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in combating scalp infections and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 part of chopped roots with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins in the herb help to reduce redness and swelling, while the antioxidants work to repair damaged skin cells and promote healthy hair growth. Additionally, pot marigold has been shown to regulate oil production in the scalp, helping to balance moisture levels and soothe irritation. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula, also known as Pot Marigold, contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and luteolin, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe an itchy, irritated scalp.

Antimicrobial: The tannins and flavonoids present in Herbal Pot Marigold Tinctures have antimicrobial properties that can help combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, reducing flakiness and dandruff.

Soothing and moisturizing: Calendula's emollient properties can help calm dryness, irritation, and itchiness, while its antioxidants work to repair and nourish the scalp, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and healthy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soothe and moisturize the scalp, reducing inflammation and flaking associated with dry scalp.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, making them effective in combating dryness and promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers, ensuring they are fresh and free from contaminants during the blooming season.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the flowers are completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after pressing out as much liquid as possible.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of dry scalp. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe itchy and irritated skin, reducing redness and flaking. Additionally, lemongrass has natural antiparasitic properties that can help to eliminate lice and other parasites that may contribute to dry scalp issues. Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tincture, such as citral and geraniol, help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itching associated with dry scalp conditions.

Antioxidant and moisturizing properties: Lemongrass tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage, while its moisturizing effects help lock in moisture to hydrate and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and flaking.

Rhizomes: As a concentrated source of essential oils, lemongrass rhizomes provide potent antifungal and antibacterial properties to combat scalp irritations and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and rinse them with clean water.
Combine the rinsed leaves with 60% to 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.
Store the final tincture in an amber glass bottle in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with dry scalp because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that soothe and calm irritated scalps. The tannins present in the leaves of the maidenhair tree tree also help to reduce dandruff and flaking, promoting a healthy scalp environment. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect the scalp from free radical damage, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in Maidenhair Tree tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness, itching, and swelling associated with a dry scalp.
Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Ginkgo biloba's antimicrobial compounds help combat infections and imbalances on the scalp that can lead to dryness and flakiness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves in early morning or late evening for optimal potency.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then pat them dry.
Combine 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 400ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least four weeks shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle discarding solids and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its unique properties. The herb's rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and mucilage creates a soothing and moisturizing effect on the scalp, calming irritation and reducing inflammation. As a result, it helps to lock in moisture, reduce flaking, and promote healthy hair growth. Additionally, fenugreek's natural antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat scalp infections, further alleviating dryness and itchiness. With regular use, herbal fenugreek tinctures can leave the scalp feeling soft, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fenugreek contains anti-inflammatory compounds like galactomannans, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Fenugreek's antifungal and antibacterial compounds can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp issues, such as dandruff and itchiness.

Moisturizing and emollient effects: Fenugreek's mucilages can provide long-lasting moisture and hydration to the scalp, soothing dryness and flakiness while also helping to lock in natural oils, promoting a healthy scalp balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots are rich in mucilage, making them an excellent ingredient for soothing and moisturizing dry scalps.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh or dried fenugreek leaves and flowers and chop them into small pieces.
Mix the chopped herbs with 4 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks allowing it to steep undisturbed.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids.
Transfer the clear liquid tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its remarkable soothing and moisturizing properties. The herb's mucilages, found in high concentrations in the root of the marshmallow plant, form a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and reducing inflammation. This natural shield also helps to calm irritated scalps, gently exfoliating and nourishing the scalp to promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming irritated scalps while reducing inflammation.
Humectant Properties: The glycoproteins in marshmallow root act as natural humectants, attracting and retaining moisture to dry scalps, promoting hydration and relief from itchiness and flakiness.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties: Marshmallow root contains antioxidants that help protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage, while its antimicrobial properties combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to a dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a natural thickening agent that helps soothe and moisturize the scalp, relieving dryness and itchiness.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis for drying.
Dry the harvested marshmallow root althaea officinalis in a single layer.
Grind the dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis into a fine powder.
Steep the ground marshmallow root althaea officinalis in vodka for tincture preparation.
Strain and filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth for final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its ability to balance hormones and reduce inflammation in the scalp. The natural oils in saw palmetto, such as beta-carotene and fatty acids, moisturize and nourish the scalp, soothing itchiness and flakiness caused by dryness. Additionally, saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and irritation, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. Regular use of saw palmetto tincture can lead to a hydrated, smooth, and vibrant hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with a dry scalp, making it easier to manage conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
Sebum-regulating properties: Saw palmetto has been shown to regulate sebum production on the scalp, which can help balance moisture levels and reduce flaking associated with dry scalps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saw palmetto plant contain beta-carotene, which helps to nourish and moisturize the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
Berries (Fruits): Saw palmetto berries are rich in fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that help to soothe and calm the scalp, promoting healthy skin and hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean dried seralnia repens leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for desired batch size.
Combine one part dried seralnia repens with four parts high-proof vodka or neutral grape spirit in a glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow for steeping.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 70 degrees.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with dry scalp because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritation and reduce flaking. The herb's natural antifungal and antibacterial properties also help combat overactive yeast on the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and itchiness. Additionally, dandelion's gentle exfoliating action removes dead skin cells, promoting a healthy scalp environment that allows for proper oil production, leaving your hair looking and feeling its best. As a result, dandelion tinctures provide a natural solution for relief from dry, itchy scalps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tinctures, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Burdock-like properties: Dandelion's bitter compounds have been shown to stimulate digestive processes and improve nutrient absorption, which can help alleviate symptoms of dry scalp by addressing underlying nutritional deficiencies or gut health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming dry scalp irritations.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin and hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for a minimum of two ounces.
Clean the herb material by gently brushing off any dirt or debris from the plant parts.
Combine the cleaned herb material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, using a ratio of one part herbs to three parts vodka.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily for maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The mucilages present in slippery elm work as a protective barrier, locking in moisture and shielding the scalp from environmental stressors. As a result, it reduces inflammation and itching, promoting a healthy balance of oil production and pH levels on the scalp. This natural remedy effectively relieves dryness, flaking, and redness, leaving the scalp feeling soft, supple, and nourished.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as mucilages, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: Slippery elm contains emollients that penetrate the skin and scalp to lock in moisture, helping to hydrate and soften dry, flaky skin.

Soil-removing and decongesting properties: Slippery elm's mucilage also acts as a gentle scrub to remove dirt, oil, and product buildup from the scalp, helping to unclog pores and promote healthy drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, slippery elm roots help soothe and calm dry scalps, reducing inflammation and irritation.
Bark: The bark's anti-inflammatory properties ease redness and discomfort associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ulmus rubra leaves and flowers in adequate quantity for the desired strength of tincture.
Cleanse the plant material by gently shaking off any debris and trimming away any stems.
Weigh 1 part of the cleaned plant material and combine it with 5 parts of 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least two weeks to allow for solvent extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle tinctures helps with dry scalp because it contains potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The antioxidant-rich extract also hydrates the scalp, providing essential moisture to alleviate dryness and itchiness. Furthermore, roselle's natural antibacterial properties help to balance the scalp's microbiome, reducing flaking and shedding. As a result, roselle tinctures can effectively restore a healthy and balanced scalp, leaving hair looking and feeling its best.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Roselle tincture helps reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can alleviate itchiness and flakiness associated with dry scalp.
Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The tincture's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dryness, itching, and flaking on the scalp.
Moisturizing and soothing properties: Roselle tincture contains natural moisturizers and emollients that help hydrate the scalp, soothe irritation, and promote healthy skin and hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, roselle roots help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Bark: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the bark of the roselle plant helps to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin and scalp development.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired tincture strength and yield.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently rinsing them under cold running water and pat drying with a paper towel.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned hibiscus sabdariffa flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar weekly to ensure proper extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and store in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood flow, thereby alleviating symptoms of dry scalp such as redness, itching, and flaking. Additionally, the antifungal and antibacterial properties of licorice tinctures help to combat underlying fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions, promoting a healthier scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which can reduce inflammation and soothe itchiness and irritation associated with a dry scalp.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice tincture's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the scalp and hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oxidative stress.

Soothing and emollient properties: Licorice root has been traditionally used as a natural emollient, providing moisture and hydration to dry, irritated scalps, making it an effective treatment for conditions like dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and moisturizing the scalp to alleviate dryness and irritation.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it an excellent addition to tinctures for dry scalp treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of glycyryrhiza glabra and cut them into small pieces weighing approximately one pound for the recipe.
Combine the cut root pieces with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering the plant material by at least two inches.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to steep for four to six weeks.
Strain the liquid from the solids through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the reserved liquid to smaller dark glass bottles and label them with the date of preparation and ingredients used keeping them stored in a cool dark place for up to one year.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry scalp are concentrated plant extracts used to treat dry, itchy, and flaky scalp conditions. They help by soothing and moisturizing the scalp, reducing inflammation, and promoting a healthy scalp environment. Effective herbal tinctures for dry scalp include Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Urtica dioica, Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe vera, Saponaria officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Equisetum arvense, and Calendula officinalis. If left untreated, dry scalp can lead to hair loss, embarrassment, and decreased self-confidence, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry scalp that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, psoriasis, and acne. These skin conditions often exhibit symptoms of flakiness, redness, and itchiness, which can be soothed by the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties found in herbal tinctures. Certain herbs such as aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil are commonly used to calm irritated skin and promote healing. By applying these tinctures topically or consuming them orally, individuals can experience relief from symptoms and improve overall skin health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with dry scalp is a blend of tea tree oil, neem oil, and rosemary oil. Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties reduce flaking and itching, while neem oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp. Rosemary oil's stimulating properties promote blood flow, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness. Mix equal parts of each oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and apply it to the scalp to experience the benefits of a healthy, flake-free scalp. Regular use can lead to improved scalp health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tincture contains compounds like cineole, which helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation. It also promotes a healthy balance of skin pH, allowing the scalp to regain its natural moisture levels. This can lead to a reduction in itchiness and flakiness, promoting a healthier and more balanced scalp environment, resulting in a smoother and more comfortable scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This compound acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to control fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dry scalp symptoms.
Linalool: As an antifungal and anti-inflammatory compound, linalool can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help soothe and treat dry scalp issues.
Barks: The bark contains a high concentration of tea tree oil, which is known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties that combat scalp infections and irritation.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia also contain tea tree oil, which is used in tinctures to help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and the plant is free from pesticides.
Dry the melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine 200 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to calm and hydrate the scalp. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and itching, while its antifungal properties combat infections that can cause dryness. Additionally, aloe vera's ability to lock in moisture helps to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment that encourages hair growth and reduces dryness.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Aloe-β-sitosterol: This phytosterol is known for its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which can help hydrate the scalp, reduce dryness, and promote a healthy scalp environment.

Aloe-6-tigloyloxy-α-hydroxyisophthalic acid (6-TAHPA): This phenolic acid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the scalp from damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of aloe vera's active compounds, particularly aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Stems: Utilized for their gel-like sap, which contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as fatty acids that help to moisturize and calm the scalp.
Leaves: Also used due to their high content of salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and exfoliating properties that help to remove dead skin cells and promote healthy scalp growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis gel from mature plants at dawn when the plant is at its highest water content.
Chop the harvested aloe barbadensis gel into small pieces and weigh out 100 grams for tincture preparation.
Combine the chopped gel with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the aloe barbadensis mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and store the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures helps with dry scalp because they contain stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp circulation, leading to improved skin health. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antifungal agents help control fungal growth, addressing the underlying cause of dry scalp. As a result, using Urtica dioica tinctures may help alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: Help reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, relieving dryness and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions.
Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): Exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat scalp irritation, reduce redness, and promote healthy scalp tissue.
Triterpenoids (Ursolic Acid): Possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections and soothe the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain urtic acid and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in treating dry scalp.
Roots: Urtica dioica roots are known to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to balance the scalp's microbiome and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in late spring or early summer when plant is in full growth.
Clean and chop the harvested urtica dioica into small pieces using sharp knives to prevent bruising.
Combine one part of the chopped urtica dioica with two parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least two weeks to allow steeping and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which combat underlying fungal infections that can cause flakiness and irritation. The herb's ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation also helps to soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, Rosmarinus officinalis has been traditionally used to improve circulation and reduce dandruff, making it a popular natural remedy for dry scalp issues. Its potent extracts help to balance scalp pH and alleviate dryness.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as an antifungal and antibacterial agent, helping to eliminate scalp infections and conditions that may contribute to dry scalp.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the scalp from damage and promote the growth of healthy hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which help to reduce inflammation and promote scalp health.
Stems: The stems contain rosmarinic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to soothe and treat dry scalp conditions.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are known for their antifungal properties, which can help to combat scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has fallen and before it gets too hot.
Chop the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems finely using a sharp knife to ensure even extraction.
Combine 1 part of the chopped rosmarinus officinalis with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The tincture, made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that nourish and calm the scalp. It helps to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and irritation. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and itchiness associated with dry scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment that allows for hair growth and a reduced risk of flaking and dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help to soothe and calm dry scalp irritation by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
Furanochromones (e.g., aloin): Act as a natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce flaking and itchiness associated with dry scalp.
Glycoproteins (e.g., acemannan): Help to strengthen the scalp's natural barrier function and promote the growth of new skin cells, reducing dryness and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside Aloe vera leaves is commonly used in tinctures for its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties to soothe and hydrate a dry scalp.
Rhyzomes: Aloe vera rhyzomes contain a high concentration of aloin, a compound that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp growth when used in tincture form.
Barks: The barks of Aloe vera plants are often used in tinctures for their emollient and soothing properties, which can help to calm and moisturize a dry, itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe vera leaves with thick fleshy skin and store them in a cool dry place for up to a week.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces weighing 1-2 pounds per gallon of solvent.
Combine the chopped aloe vera with 80-90% ethanol or glycerin in a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a clean glass container with a lid allowing for air exchange.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The herb, also known as soapwort, contains saponins that gently cleanse and nourish the scalp, removing flakes and irritation. Its emollient effects help to balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. Regular use of Saponaria officinalis tinctures may reduce dryness, itchiness, and flaking, leaving the scalp feeling soft and calm. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to scalp care routines.
remedy_constituents: Sterols: Help to moisturize and soothe the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Tannins: Act as astringents, helping to tighten and balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, reducing flaking and dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in saponins, which are known for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating scalp irritations.
Flowers: They contain saponins and flavonoids, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth.
Roots: They are a rich source of saponins, which can help to cleanse and balance the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves in late spring or early summer when they are in full bloom.
Clean the harvested plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 100 grams of the clean plant material with 500 milliliters of 25% glycerin in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the tincture to mature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tincure reduces redness and inflammation, allowing the scalp to heal. Its antiseptic properties help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dryness and itchiness. Lavandula angustifolia tinctures also have a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate scalp issues, promoting a healthy scalp and preventing dryness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antifungal properties, which can help to control fungal infections that may contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Caffeic acid: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for a dry scalp due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for a dry scalp due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce irritation and promote healthy scalp conditions.
Stems: They are used to make tinctures for a dry scalp due to their soothing and calming effects, which can help reduce itchiness and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Combine the dried flowers with 80% ethanol in a 1:2 ratio in the glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional agitation.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins present in Ginkgo biloba help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to dryness. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress may help to promote healthy hair growth and reduce flaking associated with dry scalp conditions. This natural remedy may provide relief and promote a healthier scalp. Regular use can lead to a reduction in symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the scalp, which can aid in soothing irritation and promoting healthy hair growth.
Bilobalide: Assist with dry scalp by having anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce redness and itchiness, and protect the scalp from oxidative stress.
Ginkgolides: Help with dry scalp by blocking the action of inflammatory chemicals, such as leukotrienes, which can contribute to itchiness and irritation, promoting a healthier scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part of the Ginkgo biloba plant due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are often used in traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate dry scalp conditions.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they are believed to contain compounds that can help stimulate blood flow and promote healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source for medicinal use.
Combine the dried leaves with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark location for 2 to 6 weeks to steep.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate solids.
Store the ginkgo biloba tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tincture, derived from the Horsetail plant, contains silica, which can help to strengthen hair follicles and promote healthy scalp function. Additionally, the plant's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions. By promoting a healthy balance of scalp flora and reducing inflammation, Equisetum arvense tinctures can help to alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a healthy, flake-free scalp.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry scalp by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation.
Sterols: These lipophilic compounds help moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness by maintaining the natural lipid balance of the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Equisetum arvense are used to make tinctures for dry scalp due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: The stems of Equisetum arvense are used to make tinctures for dry scalp due to their antifungal properties that help control scalp infections.
Leaves: The leaves of Equisetum arvense are used to make tinctures for dry scalp due to their astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense, cut the stems into small pieces, and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried equisetum arvense with 80 proof vodka, a 1:5 ratio, and store the mixture in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every 2 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the equisetum arvense tincture in a cool, dark place, away from children and pets, for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to calm irritation and reduce inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment. This, in turn, helps to lock in moisture, alleviate dryness and itchiness, and promote a balanced scalp microbiome. By addressing the root causes of dry scalp, Calendula officinalis tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for achieving a healthy and hydrated scalp. Regular use can lead to improved scalp health and overall hair well-being.
remedy_constituents: Naringenin: It helps with dry scalp by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and discomfort associated with scalp irritation.
Oleanolic acid: It helps with dry scalp by displaying antimicrobial properties, controlling the growth of fungi and bacteria that can exacerbate scalp conditions.
Quercetin: It helps with dry scalp by acting as an antioxidant, protecting the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the primary part used due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tinctures as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that promote scalp health and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they contain similar bioactive compounds to the flowers and leaves, aiding in scalp healing and reducing dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly within a week to preserve their potency.
Combine 1 part dried calendula flowers with 2 parts high proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it regularly to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label them including the date and ingredients for future reference.
